codex.name: Codex
codex.tagline: From pair-programmer to a team of agents you supervise.

items.title: GPT-5.3-Codex sets a new industry high
items.summary: The new flagship moves Codex beyond writing code to operating a computer end to end — topping SWE-Bench Pro and Terminal-Bench, with strong OSWorld and GDPval scores.

items.title: Codex is coming to your phone
items.summary: OpenAI says Codex will run on mobile, extending background autonomous tasks beyond the desktop so agents keep working while you're away from the keyboard.

items.title: Named a Leader in Gartner Magic Quadrant
items.summary: OpenAI is recognized as a Leader for Enterprise AI Coding Agents. Codex now reaches more than 4 million people every week.

items.title: Codex app lands on Windows
items.summary: The Codex app reframes building software — from pairing with one agent on edits to supervising coordinated teams across design, build, ship and maintain. Now available on Windows.

items.title: Sites preview: deploy apps hosted by OpenAI
items.summary: Sites lets you create, save, deploy and inspect websites, dashboards, internal tools, web apps and games — all hosted by OpenAI, straight from the Codex app.

claude.name: Claude
claude.tagline: It now writes most of its own production code.

items.title: Opus 4.8 becomes the Claude Code default
items.summary: Claude Code ships Opus 4.8 as the new default plus dynamic workflows and a security-guidance plugin, with a faster fast mode at a lower price and broad plugin, skills, hooks and background-job improvements.

items.title: 80% of Anthropic's new code authored by Claude
items.summary: More than 80% of code merged into Anthropic's production codebase is now written by Claude — up from low single digits when Claude Code launched in Feb 2025.

items.title: Code with Claude 2026: managed agents
items.summary: At its San Francisco event Anthropic unveiled managed agents, proactive workflows and the capability curve, with sessions spanning Claude Code, the Developer Platform and partner deployments.

items.title: Security plugin + faster performance
items.summary: The new security-guidance plugin monitors code edits, diffs and commits in real time to flag dangerous patterns — available to all users from the plugin marketplace.

items.title: Mythos-class models confirmed for public rollout
items.summary: Anthropic confirms its next Mythos-class models will roll out to the public, extending the coding and agentic gains seen across the Opus line.

grok.name: Grok
grok.tagline: Eight agents enter the arena. One output wins.

items.title: xAI launches Grok Build to rival Claude Code
items.summary: Grok Build is a 'powerful new coding agent and CLI for professional software engineering,' working directly from the terminal. Early beta, initially for SuperGrok Heavy subscribers.

items.title: Eight parallel agents and Arena Mode
items.summary: Grok Build runs up to eight agents at once, each through a plan → search → build workflow. Arena Mode auto-scores and ranks competing outputs before the developer reviews them.

items.title: 70.8% on SWE-Bench Verified
items.summary: The underlying model scores 70.8% on SWE-Bench Verified — putting Grok Build in the same competitive range as the leading offerings from Anthropic and OpenAI.

items.title: SuperHeavy tier at $99/mo to lure rivals
items.summary: xAI introduced a SuperHeavy tier at $99 per month for the first six months — a 67% discount aimed squarely at pulling competitors' users into the xAI ecosystem.

items.title: Musk: xAI is racing to close the coding gap
items.summary: xAI enters a crowded coding-agent race after Elon Musk admitted the company had fallen behind rivals on coding. Grok Build is its bid to catch up.

opencode.name: OpenCode
opencode.tagline: The terminal-first agent that ate the open-source harness market.

items.title: Past 167K stars — the most-used OSS coding harness
items.summary: OpenCode's 'Show HN' hit #1 within hours back in March. The star count is now past 167,000, making it the most widely used open-source coding harness.

items.title: v1.15.13: smarter adaptive reasoning
items.summary: Gateway Anthropic Opus 4.7+ adaptive reasoning now keeps summarized thinking instead of returning empty thinking blocks, and sessions can store custom metadata via API and SDK.

items.title: Passes Cline and OpenHands, closes on Aider
items.summary: Between January and April 2026 OpenCode crossed Cline, crossed OpenHands and closed the gap on Aider — despite Aider's two-year head start.

items.title: Provider-agnostic, 75+ models, polished TUI
items.summary: A Go binary that opens a native TUI, OpenCode supports multi-session work and 75+ models — Claude, OpenAI, Gemini and local — MIT-licensed and provider-agnostic from day one.

items.title: The open agent competing with Claude Code & Copilot
items.summary: InfoQ profiles OpenCode as an open-source AI coding agent challenging Claude Code and Copilot, with BYOK as the cheapest path to a Claude-powered terminal agent.
